assembledPieces.claim: The 24-bucket post-post-successor q-avoiding cover has already closed the source q-avoiding classes with zero survivors and emitted the 26-bucket post-post-post-successor surface.
Every source row still has exactly two post-post-post-successor roots, producing 545,790,988,054,702,573,768,204,062,331,322,316,786 root children before q-avoidance.

The exact q-avoiding denominator is 17,122,811,411,360,928,250,603,246,815,478,193,773,776,015 classes; singleton q157/q163 descent is blocked until this whole boundary is covered, decomposed, or replaced by an exact survivor packet.

frontierComparison.interpretation: The deterministic rank boundary consumes the 26 post-post-post-successor rank-compression decision token and records one larger q-avoiding batch-cover token. The open frontier still expands, so the next move must remain whole-boundary and theorem-facing instead of opening singleton q157/q163 descent.

supportedClaim: The post-post-post-successor 26-bucket rank partition is fully accounted as a finite theorem-facing boundary, so the only allowed next selector-shaped work is a whole-boundary q-avoiding batch cover, structural decomposition, impossibility theorem, or exact survivor boundary over all 26 buckets.

compressionCandidate.statement: Reuse the row-uniform CRT/root-count machinery from the 17-, 20-, 22-, 24-, and post-post-successor 24-bucket q-avoiding covers, starting strictly after each row's post-post-post-successor obstruction prime.
compressionCandidate.whyItMightCollapseTheRepeatedWork: The source rank-boundary packet preserves all 718 row classifications, q-avoiding counts, and post-post-post-successor obstruction primes; if every row again has a first later square obstruction, the whole 26-bucket post-post-post-successor q-avoiding surface can close without enumerating all classes.
compressionCandidate.failureBoundary: If the row-uniform cover no longer closes, emit the deterministic survivor boundary grouped by post-post-post-successor prime and previous support.

remainingPuzzleBoundary.openBoundary: The 17,122,811,411,360,928,250,603,246,815,478,193,773,776,015 post-post-post-successor q-avoiding classes are not yet covered or structurally decomposed.
remainingPuzzleBoundary.outsideClaim: This packet does not close post-post-post-successor root children, post-post-post-successor q-avoiding classes, the p479 unavailable complement, q97, p443 unavailable, the wider p4217 complement, collision-free matching, or Problem 848.

oneNextAction.action: Run the whole-boundary 26-bucket post-post-post-successor q-avoiding batch cover, or emit a deterministic exact survivor/rank boundary if it does not close.
oneNextAction.failureBoundary: If no whole-boundary cover exists, write the deterministic survivor boundary grouped by post-post-post-successor prime and prior support; do not open q157/q163 singleton descent first.
oneNextAction.whyCheaperThanSingleSelector: The entire 26-bucket post-post-post-successor surface has already been partitioned exactly and the previous q-avoiding covers closed row-uniformly, so one whole-boundary batch attempt is more informative than any singleton q child.
oneNextAction.completionRule: Every one of the 26 post-post-post-successor q-avoiding buckets is covered, structurally decomposed, or listed in a deterministic exact survivor boundary before singleton descent.

nextTheoremMove: Run the whole 26-bucket post-post-post-successor q-avoiding batch cover using the deterministic rank boundary; if it does not close, emit an exact survivor/rank boundary over all 26 buckets before singleton q-child descent.
proofBoundary: This packet is convergence assembly after the deterministic 26-bucket post-post-post-successor rank boundary. It chooses a whole 26-bucket post-post-post-successor q-avoiding cover/survivor-boundary action and blocks singleton q-child descent. It does not close any emitted root child, q-avoiding class, unavailable complement, p4217 complement, collision-free matching obligation, or Problem 848.
